name: Centerfold
classification_notes: A heavy display sans with pointed terminals and geometric underpinnings, referencing Kabel Black and 1970s print vernacular. Its single bold weight and decorative character place it firmly in the display category rather than a general-use grotesque or geometric sans.
summary: A single-weight bold display sans by Darrin Crescenzi drawing on 1970s film posters, adult magazine mastheads and classic American print advertising.
description: Structurally indebted to Rudolph Koch's Kabel Black, it pairs heavy strokes and sultry curves with pointed terminals, smoothing some of Kabel's rougher edges while adding contemporary quirks. Stylistic alternates extend its range, and the design works equally well in title case or all-caps. A period pastiche that wears its Americana references openly rather than hiding them.
foundry_blurb: Crescenzi describe Centerfold as a pastiche of various 1970s typographic references, inspired by film posters, adult magazine mastheads and classic print advertisements of the era. They note its structural resemblance to Kabel Black, with heavy weight and pointed terminals juxtaposed against sultry curves, polishing that design's rough edges with modern details. The font includes stylistic alternates and covers Latin European languages.

name: Bulward
classification_notes: An all-caps display face drawing on varsity block lettering and cast metal type traditions, placing it firmly in display; the slab-like weight and block construction situate it within a slab-inflected display subcategory.
summary: An all-caps display family of 30 styles built on the varsity block letter archetype, with softened counters and cast-metal texture giving it approachable weight.
description: Designed by Darrin Crescenzi and released in 2024, Bulward takes the varsity block letter as its starting point and layers in the slightly worn edges of cast metal type for a rougher, more characterful result. Subtly rounded counter forms temper what could be an aggressively heavy appearance, lending the face warmth alongside its considerable presence. The family ships as 30 styles with a three-axis variable font in beta, covering Latin European languages in .otf, .woff, .woff2 and .ttf formats.
foundry_blurb: Crescenzi describe Bulward as inspired by the varsity block archetype and the worn edges of cast metal type, made fresh through a rough-hewn and quirky construction. They note its aura of tradition and steadfastness offset by gently rounded counter forms, and position it across uses as varied as sports jerseys and chainsaw blade packaging. It is an all-caps display face with wide Latin language support across 30 styles, released with a three-axis variable font in beta.

name: Bulward
classification_notes: An all-caps varsity block-inspired display face with slab-like construction and rough-hewn edges; its heritage in cast metal type and sports lettering places it firmly in the display category.
summary: An all-caps display family by Darrin Crescenzi drawing on varsity block lettering and the worn edges of cast metal type, available in 30 styles across a 3-axis variable font.
description: Inspired by the varsity block archetype, Bulward combines a rough-hewn construction with subtly rounded counter forms that temper its heaviness. The family ships in 30 styles and as a 3-axis variable font, covering a wide range of expressive weight and width combinations while maintaining a consistent visual character. Suited to sports jerseys, packaging and any context where a coarse, workwear aesthetic is wanted.
foundry_blurb: Crescenzi describe Bulward as a font family inspired by the varsity block archetype and the worn edges of cast metal type, noting that its rounded counter forms soften its solid appearance and give it warmth. They position it as an all-caps display face equally at home on sports jerseys and chainsaw blade packaging, with 30 styles and a 3-axis variable font offering broad expressive range.
